skills.name: shadcn/ui Component Installation and Usage
skills.description: Install and use shadcn/ui components in a Next.js or React project. Components are copied into your codebase, not imported from a package.
skills.template:
# shadcn/ui Installation ## Setup ```bash # Initialize shadcn/ui in your project npx shadcn@latest init # Add components individually npx shadcn@latest add button npx shadcn@latest add dialog npx shadcn@latest add form npx shadcn@latest add table ``` ## Component Usage ```tsx // components/ui/button is OWNED by your project — edit freely import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; export function Example() { return (
); } ``` ## Available Variants (Button) - `default` — primary brand color - `destructive` — red, for delete actions - `outline` — bordered, no fill - `secondary` — muted background - `ghost` — no background, hover only - `link` — looks like a hyperlink ## Sizes - `default`, `sm`, `lg`, `icon` ## Key Principle All shadcn/ui components live in `components/ui/`. You own the source — customize without fear of upstream breaking changes.

skills.name: cn() Utility — clsx + tailwind-merge
skills.description: The cn() helper merges Tailwind classes safely, resolving conflicts (e.g. p-4 + p-2 → p-2) and conditionally applying classes.

skills.name: Tailwind CSS v4 — New Syntax and Features
skills.description: Tailwind CSS v4 moves configuration into CSS using @theme, drops tailwind.config.js for most use cases, and introduces new utility syntax.

skills.name: shadcn/ui Theming and Dark Mode
skills.description: shadcn/ui uses CSS custom properties for theming. Dark mode is configured via class strategy and toggled with next-themes.

skills.name: Class Variance Authority (CVA) for Component Variants
skills.description: CVA provides type-safe variant management for components, used internally by shadcn/ui and useful for custom design system components.

skills.name: shadcn/ui Form with react-hook-form + Zod
skills.description: Complete form pattern using shadcn/ui Form components, react-hook-form for state, and Zod for schema validation.

skills.name: shadcn/ui Dialog, Sheet, and Drawer Patterns
skills.description: Accessible modal overlays using shadcn/ui Dialog (centered), Sheet (side panel), and Drawer (bottom sheet on mobile).

skills.name: shadcn/ui Table with TanStack Table
skills.description: Data table pattern combining shadcn/ui Table primitives with TanStack Table for sorting, filtering, and pagination.

skills.name: Composing Radix UI Primitives
skills.description: shadcn/ui is built on Radix UI primitives. Understanding Radix lets you build custom accessible components beyond the shadcn preset.
skills.template:
# Radix UI Primitives

```bash
npm install @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu
npm install @radix-ui/react-tooltip
npm install @radix-ui/react-popover
```

## Custom Dropdown (Radix + Tailwind, no shadcn preset)
```tsx
import * as DropdownMenu from '@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';

function ActionMenu({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <DropdownMenu.Root>
      <DropdownMenu.Trigger asChild>{children}</DropdownMenu.Trigger>
      <DropdownMenu.Portal>
        <DropdownMenu.Content
          className={cn(
            'z-50 min-w-32 rounded-md border bg-popover p-1 shadow-md',
            'data-[state=open]:animate-in data-[state=closed]:animate-out',
            'data-[state=closed]:fade-out-0 data-[state=open]:fade-in-0',
          )}
          sideOffset={4}
        >
          <DropdownMenu.Item className="flex cursor-pointer items-center rounded-sm px-2 py-1.5 text-sm outline-none hover:bg-accent">
            Edit
          </DropdownMenu.Item>
          <DropdownMenu.Separator className="-mx-1 my-1 h-px bg-muted" />
          <DropdownMenu.Item className="flex cursor-pointer items-center rounded-sm px-2 py-1.5 text-sm text-destructive outline-none hover:bg-accent">
            Delete
          </DropdownMenu.Item>
        </DropdownMenu.Content>
      </DropdownMenu.Portal>
    </DropdownMenu.Root>
  );
}
```

## Key Radix Concepts
- **asChild**: merge Radix props onto your element, no extra DOM node
- **Portal**: renders outside current DOM tree (avoids z-index/overflow issues)
- **data-[state]**: Radix sets state attributes — use in Tailwind for enter/exit animations
- **Compound pattern**: Root → Trigger → Content → Item hierarchy

## Accessibility is automatic
Radix handles: focus management, keyboard nav, ARIA roles, screen reader announcements.

skills.name: Tailwind Fluid Typography and Responsive Design
skills.description: Using clamp() for fluid sizing, Tailwind breakpoints, and modern responsive patterns without media query proliferation.
skills.template:
# Tailwind Responsive + Fluid Design ## Fluid Typography with clamp() ```css /* globals.css */ @theme { --text-hero: clamp(2.5rem, 1rem + 6vw, 6rem); --text-section: clamp(1.5rem, 0.5rem + 3vw, 3rem); --text-body: clamp(1rem, 0.875rem + 0.5vw, 1.125rem); } ``` ```html

Hero

Section title

``` ## Responsive Breakpoints ```html
``` ## Container Queries (prefer over breakpoints for components) ```html

Title

``` ## Space Scale with clamp() ```css @theme { --space-section: clamp(3rem, 2rem + 5vw, 8rem); --space-component: clamp(1.5rem, 1rem + 2vw, 3rem); } ``` ```html
...
``` ## Avoid - Hardcoded pixel sizes for typography - Viewport-dependent breakpoints for reusable components - `sm:`, `md:` in component internals (use `@container` instead)
